sections.paragraph:
not used in Kal.

Niphal-

(1) to lean upon, to rest upon, as a spear, followed by עַל 2Sa 1:6. נִשְׁעַן עַל יַד פּ׳ to lean on any one’s hand, spoken of kings, who were accustomed to go in public leaning on their friends and ministers, 2Ki 5:18 7:2 , 17 2Ki 7:17. Metaph. to repose confidence in any person or thing, followed by עַל Isa 10:20 31:1 Job 8:15 2Ch 13:18 14:10 16:7 Mic 3:11 followed by אֶל Pro 3:5 followed by בְּ Isa 50:10 absol. Job, 24:23 .

(2) to lean against, followed by עַל, Jud. 16:26 of a country, followed by לְ Num 21:15.

(3) to recline (prop. to rest upon the elbow), Gen 18:4.

Derivatives, מִשְׁעָן, מִשְׁעֶנֶת, מַשְׁעֵן, and pr.n. אֶשְׁעָן.
